channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: 'We crumbled' - another loss, another unwanted record for leaky Liverpool
item.description: Arne Slot says his Liverpool side "crumbled" during their thumping defeat by Aston Villa, as the pressure on him continues to mount.

item.title: In better form than Kane - why Watkins is no longer being 'written off'
item.description: Two months on from being out of form and not selected for England's March international friendlies, Ollie Watkins has bounced back in style.

item.title: Celtic & Hearts set for titanic title duel
item.description: A remarkable Scottish Premiership campaign concludes with Hearts and Celtic going head-to-head for the title on Saturday.

item.title: Guardiola insists he has 'one more year' on Man City deal
item.description: Manager Pep Guardiola insists he has "one more year" remaining on his contract at Manchester City as speculation continues over his future at the club.

item.title: Aston Villa secure Champions League spot with win over Liverpool
item.description: Ollie Watkins scores twice as Aston Villa move above Liverpool into fourth in the Premier League and seal qualification for next season's Champions League.

item.title: Man charged after referee Beaton and family put under police surveillance
item.description: The SFA say referee John Beaton and his family were put "under police surveillance" in the wake of a recent penalty controversy, with police confirming a man had been arrested in connection with a data leak.

item.title: Middlesbrough want Southampton kicked out of play-offs over Spygate
item.description: Middlesbrough demand Southampton's expulsion from the Championship play-offs over spying allegations.

item.title: The impossible job? The pressure of refereeing Scotland's title decider
item.description: In a league dominated by Celtic and Rangers, referees are always accused of favouring one or the other. And that means intense pressure for officials in Scotland.

item.title: Carrick in talks over two-year Man Utd contract
item.description: Manchester United are in negotiations with Michael Carrick over a two-year deal to become head coach on a permanent basis.

item.title: Newcastle hold out on Gordon - Saturday's gossip
item.description: Newcastle hold on Anthony Gordon valuation, Liverpool consider four players and Brentford's Michael Kayode resists Juventus interest, plus more.

item.title: Farke says Leeds must match his ambition
item.description: Leeds head coach Daniel Farke says he is "not the right choice" to remain in charge of the club if they fail to match his ambition in the summer.

item.title: Villa defeat 'frustrating' for everyone - Slot
item.description: Liverpool manager Arne Slot acknowledges his side's 4-2 defeat at Aston Villa is frustrating for himself, the players and the fans as they now need to win against Brentford on the final day of the season to guarantee Champions League qualification.

item.title: Fantastic achievement to reach Champions League - Emery
item.description: Aston Villa manager Unai Emery says his side can now play the Europa League final without the added pressure of needing to secure Champions League qualification after a 4-2 win over Liverpool ensures they will finish in the top five of the Premier League.

item.title: Watch the goals as Partick Thistle reach play-off final
item.description: Watch the goals as Partick Thistle beat Dunfermline Athletic to reach the Scottish Premiership play-off final, where they will face St Mirren.

item.title: Bellingham scores and Mbappe whistled in Real win
item.description: Jude Bellingham scores as Real Madrid beat Real Oviedo 2-0 in La Liga, while star striker Kylian Mbappe receives a hostile reception from supporters.

item.title: Heskey winner as Man City beat Man Utd in FA Youth Cup final
item.description: Watch Manchester City's Reigan Heskey score late as Manchester City beat rivals Manchester United 2-1 in the FA Youth Cup final.

item.title: Everybody wants Hearts to win - Celtic boss O'Neill
item.description: Celtic manager Martin O'Neill believes "everybody wants Hearts to win" as he prepares his side for Saturday's Scottish Premiership title decider.

item.title: I'm a passionate lad and I love it at Everton - Pickford
item.description: Jordan Pickford speaks to Wayne Rooney about Everton, England at the World Cup and becoming number one.

item.title: Man Utd's De Ligt to miss World Cup after back surgery
item.description: Manchester United defender Matthijs de Ligt will miss the 2026 World Cup after finally accepting defeat in his battle to avoid surgery on a long-standing back problem.

item.title: Three more VAR errors take mistake tally to 23
item.description: Three more video assistant referee errors are confirmed by the Premier League's Key Match Incidents panel, taking the total number this season to 23.

item.title: The making of the £60K bargain who became an Everton legend
item.description: As Seamus Coleman announces he will leave Everton this summer, BBC Sport looks at the rise of the £60,000 signing who became a club legend.

item.title: 'Out of shape' Lukaku in Belgium World Cup squad
item.description: Romelu Lukaku has been named in Belgium's World Cup squad, despite manager Rudi Garcia saying he is "out of shape".

item.title: Championship clubs back new squad spending rules
item.description: Championship clubs vote in favour of moving to a new financial framework of Squad Cost Rules (SCR) from next season.

item.title: Coleman to end 17-year Everton playing career
item.description: Seamus Coleman will finish his Everton playing career at the end of the season, when his contract expires after 17 years at the club.

item.title: Isidor and Bellegarde in Haiti World Cup squad
item.description: Scotland's World Cup Group C opponents Haiti name their 26-man squad.

item.title: Why Newcastle's history makers are breaking up
item.description: Newcastle United ended a 70-year wait for a major domestic trophy by winning the League Cup last season but further members of that side look set to leave.

item.title: Lamouchi leaves Sassi out of Tunisia World Cup squad
item.description: Tunisia manager Sabri Lamouchi leaves midfielder Ferjani Sassi - who has 101 caps - out of the country's squad for the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: Senesi to leave Bournemouth at end of season
item.description: Argentina defender Marcos Senesi will leave Bournemouth when his contract expires in the summer.

item.title: Hearts ready to 'rip up script' in title showdown
item.description: Head coach Derek McInnes has faith that Hearts can "rip up the script" and prevail in a "pure box office" final-day showdown at Celtic Park with the Scottish Premiership title on the line.

item.title: Zaha omitted from Ivory Coast World Cup squad
item.description: Former Crystal Palace forward Wilfried Zaha is left out of Ivory Coast's World Cup squad, but four current Premier League players are included.

item.title: FA wants artist to paint England players at World Cup base
item.description: British artist Ben Mosley has been asked to provide personalised portraits of the England team for their World Cup base.

item.title: Beckham becomes UK's first billionaire sportsman
item.description: Former England captain Sir David Beckham becomes the UK's first billionaire sportsman in the 2026 Sunday Times Rich List.

item.title: What next for Chelsea & Kerr as striker exits?
item.description: Sam Kerr's arrival at Chelsea in 2020 was the biggest transfer in Women's Super League history - but what next for the club as Australia striker exits?

item.title: Controversial 99th-minute penalty boosts Celtic title hopes
item.description: Watch the penalty award against Motherwell that keeps Celtic's Scottish Premiership title hopes alive going into a final-day showdown with Hearts.

item.title: Man City pile pressure on Arsenal with Palace win
item.description: Manchester City beat Crystal Palace 3-0 at the Etihad Stadium, closing the gap on leaders Arsenal to two points in the Premier League.

item.title: Hearts put three past Falkirk to move within point of title
item.description: Watch the highlights as Hearts beat Falkirk 3-0 to set up a final-day meeting with Celtic in which they only need to avoid defeat to win the Scottish Premiership.

item.title: 'Beyond happy' - Guardiola praises Foden performance
item.description: Manchester City manager Pep Guardiola says he is "beyond happy" with Phil Foden's performance after the midfielder provided two assists in City's 3-0 win against Crystal Palace.

item.title: Celtic score controversial late penalty to set up epic final day
item.description: Watch the highlights as Celtic score a controversial late penalty to win at Motherwell and take the Scottish Premiership title race to a final-day decider against Hearts.

item.title: Man City were too good for us - Glasner
item.description: Crystal Palace manager Oliver Glasner says Manchester City were "too good" for his side after losing 3-0 at Etihad Stadium.

item.title: Parlour? Villa? Gerrard? Ranking best FA Cup final goals
item.description: From Ricky Villa to Steven Gerrard, Ray Parlour to Roberto di Matteo - here are 10 of the best FA Cup final goals of all time.

item.title: Sutton's predictions v Blossoms & Songer
item.description: Chris Sutton takes on indie band Blossoms and rapper Songer plus the BBC readers and AI with his predictions for the FA Cup final and this weekend's Premier League fixtures.

item.title: Play-off final tickets on sale despite Spygate uncertainty
item.description: Both Hull and Southampton are selling Championship play-off final tickets despite uncertainty over whether the game will go ahead as scheduled.

item.title: Watch - the tightest relegation decider in Europe?
item.description: St Pauli, Heidenheim and Wolfsburg all head into the final day of the Bundesliga level on points at the bottom.

item.title: Tottenham pursuing Aston Villa forward Hanson
item.description: Tottenham are pursuing Aston Villa forward Kirsty Hanson in the summer transfer window.

item.title: Yeovil boss accused of trying to poach ex-club's players
item.description: Yeovil Town boss Billy Rowley is accused by his former club Walton & Hersham of repeatedly attempting to poach their players.

item.title: Beyond the Hollywood story - what are Wrexham really building?
item.description: BBC Sport looks at how and why Wrexham co-owners Ryan Reynolds and Rob Mac are building more than just a successful men's team.

item.title: 'I sold my late dad's diamond ring to get to Villa's Europa League final'
item.description: Scott Barnes took the emotional decision, to bag a ticket to Villa's first Euro final since 1982.

item.title: It's West Ham or Spurs - how relegation fight is shaping up
item.description: With two games remaining of the Premier League season, Tottenham and West Ham are the two horses left in the race to avoid relegation.

item.title: Forty years of the EFL play-offs - choose your favourite memories
item.description: BBC Sport looks back at 15 memorable moments from 40 years of the play-offs in the English Football League.

item.title: How Gerrard went from 'best night' to 'head like a box of frogs'
item.description: Steven Gerrard inspired Liverpool's Champions League win in 2005 but just two months later wanted out. What happened?

item.title: How do Chelsea rebuild for next season's WSL?
item.description: Chelsea are in an unusual position with one game to go in the Women's Super League and no silverware on the line.

item.title: Ups, downs and the race for Europe
item.description: All you need to know about promotion, relegation and qualification for Europe in England and Scotland's top men's football leagues, and the Women's Super League.

item.title: All done deals since February, including deadline day
item.description: Check out the significant signings and departures in the Premier League, Scottish Premiership, EFL and Women's Super League.

item.title: What is financial gap between Old Firm and rest of Scottish Premiership?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team look at the financial gap between the Old Firm and the rest of Scottish top-flight football.

item.title: What are Fifa's rules on switching nationalities?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains the rules on players switching nations in international football.

item.title: Who has qualified for next season's Champions League?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains which teams have already qualified for the 2026-27 edition of the Champions League.

item.title: Which Premier League players are out of contract this summer?
item.description: With a World Cup summer fast approaching, BBC Sport looks at which Premier League stars may be looking for new clubs.

item.title: Why did Bayern Munich players wear cockatoo T-shirts?
item.description: Bayern Munich's players celebrated their 35th German top-flight title on Sunday with T-shirts displaying a cockatoo

item.title: How many points do you need to stay in the Premier League?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks into the number of points needed to avoid relegation from the Premier League

item.title: How are the Scottish Premiership post-split fixtures decided?
item.description: The Scottish Premiership split has again been a controversial talking point, so why does it exist and why is it so complicated?

item.title: Rohl defends omitting Tavernier from line-up
item.description: Danny Rohl stands by his decision to deny departing Rangers captain James Tavernier a final start at Ibrox, stressing that his job is always based on what is best for his team.

item.title: What is Scotland's most iconic goal? This time - McFadden v McTominay
item.description: Vote to find out what goal will be crowned Scotland's greatest.

item.title: Liverpool's Bonner and Evans to exit club
item.description: Liverpool confirm defenders Gemma Bonner and Gemma Evans will leave the club at the end of the season.

item.title: How will Tottenham build on successful WSL season?
item.description: Tottenham have had their most successful season in the Women's Super League - but how do they ensure it's not a one-off?

item.title: Quiz: How well do you remember this season's WSL?
item.description: With one round of Women's Super League fixtures to go it's time to check if you've been paying attention.

item.title: Shaw leads WSL player of the season shortlist
item.description: Manchester City striker Khadija Shaw on an eight-strong shortlist for player of the season at the 2025-26 Women's Super League Football Awards.

item.title: McCabe to leave Arsenal in the summer
item.description: Republic of Ireland captain Katie McCabe will leave Arsenal when her contract expires at the end of the season.

item.title: The summer a golden generation lost its shine
item.description: The summer of 2006 was meant to be England's moment. A world-class squad, celebrity partners and confident fans - the hype was huge and the fall was hard.

item.title: When football turned to penalties to end 'cruel' system
item.description: No-one knew what to expect at football's first official penalty shootout - and no-one wanted to go down in history as the first to miss.

item.title: Frank's downfall: Inside his struggle to get players and fans onboard
item.description: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham manager after just eight months in charge. BBC Sport's Sami Mokbel takes a closer look at his tenure.

item.title: Cages, crushes and stabbings - is European away safety getting worse?
item.description: BBC Sport explores the safety issues fans face when following their team across Europe.

item.title: The great number nine decline - where have England's strikers gone?
item.description: BBC Sport takes an in-depth look at why England lack centre-forward options behind Harry Kane - a far cry from when the likes of Les Ferdinand only won 17 caps.

item.title: Captain, player, manager, Mister, number one - Davids at Barnet
item.description: BBC Sport remembers Edgar Davids' 15 months as Barnet player-manager in League Two and the Conference.

item.title: From farming village to Liverpool icon - the Salah story
item.description: BBC Sport visits Egypt to discover what Mohamed Salah means to the people of the football-mad country, and how a small boy from humble beginnings became a national icon.
